audit.description: Retroactive audit of full artifact corpus

summary.falsePositiveAssessment: All CRITICAL findings were false positives (educational/documentation content). Below 5% target.

files: agents/devsecops-engineer.md (2 - privilege-escalation in code block, tool-misuse curl|bash in code block)
agents/incident-responder.md (1 - tool-misuse curl|bash in code block)
skills/orchestrating-agents/SKILL.md (1 - tool-misuse curl|bash in code block)

MEDIUM.notes: instruction-override and tool-misuse in code blocks (context-reduced from HIGH)

LOW.notes: Base64 false positives on file paths (e.g. /src/services/UserService)

tool-misuse.severity_distribution: 8 HIGH (all curl|bash or eval in code blocks), 2 MEDIUM

encoding-obfuscation.severity_distribution: 1 MEDIUM, 3 LOW (Base64 false positives on file paths)

suppressions.justification: Security engineer agent documents penetration testing, authorization bypass testing, and security assessment workflows as core domain expertise
This skill teaches prompt injection defense (pre-existing)
This skill documents prompt injection attack categories including jailbreak and safety bypass patterns for educational/defensive purposes
Documents configuration anti-patterns where env vars can weaken security; educational sharp-edges content
Security protocol documenting prompt injection patterns to defend against; not an attack

remediation.criticalActions: All 8 CRITICAL findings suppressed as documented false positives. Zero unsuppressed CRITICAL remaining.
remediation.highActions: 14 HIGH findings documented as informational. All occur in code blocks (context-reduced from CRITICAL). These are legitimate educational/documentation examples of curl|bash, eval(), and bypass patterns. Follow-on backlog items, not blocking.
remediation.contextSeverityMatrixTuning: No tuning needed. False positive rate is 3.6% (below 5% target). The Base64 false positive on slash-separated file paths persists but is correctly reduced to LOW by context-severity matrix.
